Artifact.description:
A multimodal artifact attached to a chat turn.

Artifacts are normalized content blocks (images, documents, audio, video)
that flow through the agent alongside the user's text message. The
normalizer in ``api.py`` converts these to LangChain content blocks
before passing them to the agent.

One of ``data`` (base64) or ``url`` must be set, but not both.

filename.description: Original filename for display / TUI metadata.
filename.title: Filename

url.description: Public URL to the artifact. Mutually exclusive with ``data``.
url.title: Url

Artifact.title: Artifact

ArtifactInfo.description:
Lightweight artifact metadata for TUI rendering and thread history.

Does not carry the raw binary data — only metadata needed to render an
artifact entry in the TUI (filename, type, analysis state) and to drive
the analysis lifecycle.

analysis_result.description: Short text result from multimodal analysis, once completed.
analysis_result.title: Analysis Result

id.description: Unique artifact identifier within the thread.
id.title: Id

ArtifactInfo.title: ArtifactInfo

AuditSection.description: A grouped batch of checks (memory / process / fix-verifications / runtime / langsmith).
AuditSection.title: AuditSection

ChatMessage.description: One message in a thread, normalized for the wire.

artifacts.description: Artifact metadata for multimodal content in this message.
artifacts.title: Artifacts

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage

ChatRequest.description: Inbound payload for a single chat turn.

thread_id.description: Optional thread id. Server scopes it to the authenticated caller.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest

ChatResponse.description: Response to a non-streaming chat request.
ChatResponse.title: ChatResponse

CheckResult.description: One named check inside an audit section.
CheckResult.title: CheckResult

EvalQueryResult.description: One graded query in an eval report.
EvalQueryResult.title: EvalQueryResult

skip_reason.description: Human-readable reason for skipping, set when skipped=true.
skip_reason.title: Skip Reason

skipped.description: True when the eval did not run (e.g. provider API key missing). Distinct from ok=false, which means the run completed below the pass-rate threshold.
skipped.title: Skipped

EvalRequest.description:
Inbound payload for POST /v1/eval.

The golden dataset is hardcoded to ``tests/golden_dataset.json``
— no user-controlled path reaches the filesystem. Only the pass
rate threshold is configurable from the client.

min_pass_rate.description: Minimum pass rate (0.0-1.0) below which the report is marked not ok.
min_pass_rate.title: Min Pass Rate

EvalRequest.title: EvalRequest

MemoryFile.description:
A single file in the agent's ``/memories/`` or ``/policies/`` filesystem.

Returned by ``GET /v1/memories/{path}`` and ``GET /v1/policies/{path}``.
Read-only debug surface for inspecting the agent-scoped memory
file (e.g. ``AGENTS.md``) and the shared policy files. The
``content`` field is the raw FileData content — multi-line string
for plain text files, JSON-encoded blob for binary.

content.description: File body as UTF-8 string. Empty string when the file is missing or unreadable.
content.title: Content

exists.description: True when the file was found in the store.
exists.title: Exists

namespace.description: Resolved LangGraph store namespace for this read. Per-caller for ``/v1/memories/*`` (matches the agent's view), shared ``ossia/policies`` for ``/v1/policies/*``.
namespace.title: Namespace

path.description: Path relative to the route root, e.g. ``AGENTS.md`` for ``/v1/memories/AGENTS.md``.
path.title: Path

MemoryFile.title: MemoryFile

PluginInfo.description:
A plugin that the running agent has loaded.

Surface name, source file, and what it contributed. Used by
``GET /v1/plugins`` for diagnostics. Ponytail: fields mirror
``core.plugin.LoadedPlugin`` — no second class to keep in sync.

config.description: Config dict from ossia.json passed to register(api, config=...).
config.title: Config

middleware_types.description: Middleware class names this plugin registered.
middleware_types.title: Middleware Types

module.description: Import alias used to load the plugin (e.g. ossia_plugin_ponytail).
module.title: Module

path.description: Filesystem path of the plugin entry file.
path.title: Path

PluginInfo.title: PluginInfo

ResumeDecision.description:
A single reviewer's decision on a pending action request.

Mirrors the DeepAgents HITL ``Command(resume={"decisions": [...]})`` shape;
one decision per ``action_request`` surfaced via ``result.interrupts``.

Decision types:
    - ``approve``: execute the tool call as-is.
    - ``edit``:    replace the action with ``edited_action`` (must include
      ``name`` and ``args``) and execute that instead.
    - ``reject``:  do not execute; the optional ``message`` is forwarded
      to the agent as feedback.
    - ``respond``: do not execute; the optional ``message`` is returned
      to the agent as a successful tool result (use only when the tool
      is intentionally a placeholder for human input).

edited_action.description: Required when type='edit'; the replacement action (name, args).
edited_action.title: Edited Action

message.description: Optional reviewer message. For type='reject' it is added to the conversation as feedback. For type='respond' it is returned as the tool result. Ignored for type='approve' and (when omitted) for type='edit'.
message.title: Message

ResumeDecision.title: ResumeDecision

ResumeRequest.description:
Inbound payload for resuming a thread paused on a human-review interrupt.

Maps to ``agent.invoke(Command(resume={"decisions": [...]}), config, version="v2")``.
Decisions are applied to the pending ``action_requests`` in the order
they were surfaced by the run that produced the interrupt.

decisions.description: One decision per pending action request, in the order surfaced.
decisions.title: Decisions

ResumeRequest.title: ResumeRequest

ResumeResponse.description: Result of resuming a thread. May itself contain fresh interrupts.

feedback.description: Optional reviewer feedback forwarded to the agent on reject.
feedback.title: Feedback

interrupted.description: True when the resumed run paused on a new interrupt.
interrupted.title: Interrupted

ResumeResponse.title: ResumeResponse

ThreadEventsResponse.description: A thread's buffered normalized event stream.

count.description: Number of events in the response.
count.title: Count

events.description: Normalized OssiaEvent dicts in emit order.
events.title: Events

ThreadEventsResponse.title: ThreadEventsResponse

ThreadStateResponse.description: A thread's current graph state (values + next nodes).
ThreadStateResponse.title: ThreadStateResponse

ToolCall.description: A single tool invocation produced by the model.
ToolCall.title: ToolCall

ToolInfo.description: A tool the running agent has loaded.

server.description: MCP server name, set only when source='mcp'.
server.title: Server

ToolInfo.title: ToolInfo

WebhookCreate.description:
Request payload for POST /v1/webhooks.

``events`` is a list of event kinds (e.g. ``["message", "tool_call"]``)
or ``["*"]`` to subscribe to everything. ``secret`` is optional;
when omitted the server generates one and returns it once in the
response. The receiver should store the secret and verify the
``X-Ossia-Signature`` header on every delivery.

events.description: Event kinds to subscribe to. '*' matches all.
events.title: Events

secret.description: Optional HMAC secret. Server generates one when empty.
secret.title: Secret

url.description: HTTP(S) URL the server POSTs events to.
url.title: Url

WebhookCreate.title: WebhookCreate

WebhookCreated.description:
Response to POST /v1/webhooks.

Includes the secret once. The secret is the only chance the
caller has to copy it — subsequent GETs redact it. Ponytail:
no Redis / DB persistence in v0.1, so a server restart drops
the secret; the webhook still works, but the receiver cannot
verify signatures. The endpoint returns 200 with the secret
inline so a fresh install can capture it.

secret.description: HMAC secret used to sign deliveries. Returned once.
secret.title: Secret

WebhookCreated.title: WebhookCreated

WebhookInfo.description: A registered webhook (public view, no secret).
WebhookInfo.title: WebhookInfo

WhoAmIResponse.description:
Response payload for GET /v1/whoami.

Returns the stable caller id derived from the presented API
key. Useful for clients to confirm which credential the
server saw and to debug multi-tenant setups. The ``key_fpr``
field is the first 8 chars of the Argon2id of the key — a
fingerprint the caller can use to label which key they sent
without revealing the secret.

caller.description: Stable caller id used for thread scoping and audit logs.
caller.title: Caller

key_fpr.description: Short fingerprint of the presented key (first 8 hex chars).
key_fpr.title: Key Fpr

WhoAmIResponse.title: WhoAmIResponse

info.description: Unified HTTP API for the Ossia support agent.
info.title: Ossia Support Agent

get.description: Run the in-process audit harness and return a structured report.

post.description: Run a single chat turn against the agent.

Stream a chat turn as Server-Sent Events, v3 protocol.

Built on ``agent.astream_events(..., version="v3")``. The v3 stream
is caller-driven: the handler iterates the typed projections
(``messages``, ``tool_calls``, ``subagents``, ``values``) concurrently
and yields a :class:`StreamEvent` per projection update. The SSE
``event:`` field is the ``kind`` discriminator, so clients can
subscribe to specific kinds via
``EventSource.addEventListener("message", ...)``.

A final ``kind="complete"`` event is always sent. ``interrupted=true``
in that event means the run paused on a human-review interrupt;
the client should ``POST /v1/threads/{id}/resume`` to continue.

The v3 streaming protocol is marked experimental by langgraph; if
the upstream shape changes, only the projection adapters below need
to update — the wire contract (``kind`` + per-kind ``data`` shape)
is the part we promise to clients.
Run the golden-dataset eval and return a graded report.

get.description:
Read a file from the agent's ``/memories/`` filesystem.

Read-only debug surface for inspecting the agent's memory
(e.g. ``GET /v1/memories/AGENTS.md``). Returns the file body as
a UTF-8 string with ``exists=false`` when the key is absent.
The namespace mirrors the agent's: per-caller by default, agent
scope when ``Settings.memory_scope='agent'``.

List plugins the running agent has loaded, with provenance and config.

Plugin discovery is idempotent (modules cached in ``sys.modules``),
so calling ``discover_plugins`` on every request is cheap. The
result reflects whatever ``ossia.json`` and the bundled + user
plugin dirs look like at request time.

Read a file from the shared ``/policies/`` filesystem.

Policy files are populated by application code at startup via
``seed_policy`` and protected by a write-deny permission. This
route is the read-only inspection surface (e.g. for verifying
that compliance content reached the agent). All callers see the
same namespace ``("ossia", "policies")``.

delete.description:
Clear the buffered normalized event stream for a thread.

Removes all buffered events for the thread from the in-memory buffer.
Subsequent ``GET /v1/threads/{id}/events`` will return an empty list.

get.description:
Return the buffered normalized event stream for a thread.

Events are stored in-memory after each ``POST /v1/chat/stream`` completes.
Clients can use this endpoint to replay or late-join a thread's event
stream for debugging, audit, or TUI session recovery.

Only threads that have had at least one streaming invocation since server
start will have buffered events. The buffer is bounded per-thread to
prevent unbounded memory growth (~5 MB max per thread).

Returns:
    A ``ThreadEventsResponse`` containing the ordered list of normalized
    ``OssiaEvent`` dicts and the event count.
Return flattened message history for a thread, newest first.

post.description:
Resume a thread paused on a human-review interrupt.

Maps to ``agent.invoke(Command(resume={"decisions": [...]}), config, version="v2")``.
Each decision's optional ``message`` is passed through to the agent as
described on :class:`ResumeDecision`; there is no top-level ``feedback``
field (the upstream API does not define one).

get.description: Return the latest checkpointed state for a thread.
List tools the running agent has loaded, with provenance.

post.description:
Register a webhook to receive thread events.

Delivery is best-effort: 3 attempts with exponential backoff,
HMAC-SHA256 signature on the ``X-Ossia-Signature`` header.
The secret is returned once in this response — store it
server-side; subsequent GETs redact it.

schema.title: Response Delete Webhook V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Delete

get.description:
Identify the caller.

Returns the stable ``caller`` id the server uses to scope
threads and audit logs, plus a short fingerprint of the
presented key so the caller can verify which key the server
saw (handy in multi-key deployments).
